name: Nuaman ibn Amr
title: Devoted Brother
shortDescription: A true Shia of Kufa who rushed to assist Imam Hussain (AS) along with his brother Hallas and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Nu'man ibn Amr al-Rasibi, along with his brother Hallas ibn Amr, were renowned and loyal Shias of Kufa. They were among the special companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) in Kufa and served in the Shurtat al-Khamis (elite forces) during the Battle of Siffin.

Nu'man and his brother, who had spent their lives defending Wilayah, could not bear to see the Master of Martyrs (AS) alone. They secretly fled Kufa at night and joined the Imam's caravan in Karbala before the roads were blocked.

On Ashura, these two old and experienced brothers enthusiastically went to the battlefield to defend the Prophet's (PBUH) sanctuary. Nu'man fought valiantly during the enemy's shower of arrows on the morning of Ashura (first attack) and attained the exalted rank of martyrdom shoulder-to-shoulder with his brother.

The infallible Imam's greeting in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya and Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya is the reward for his lifetime of sincere struggle.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى النُّعْمانِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الرّاسِبِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Nu'man ibn Amr al-Rasibi...
